everyone.name: Abigail Bangser
everyone.bio: Abby is a Principal Engineer at Syntasso delivering Kratix, an open-source cloud-native framework for building internal platforms on Kubernetes. Her keen interest in supporting internal development comes from over a decade of experience in consulting and product delivery roles across platform, site reliability, and quality engineering.  Abby is an international keynote speaker, Team Topologies Advocate, and co-host of the #CoffeeOps London meetup. Outside of work, Abby spoils her pup Zino and enjoys playing team sports.

everyone.name: Faseela K
everyone.bio: Faseela is a cloud-native developer, maintainer and steering committee member at Istio Service Mesh. She has served as the Program Chair for the Istio Meetups in 2023 including the IstioDays co-located with KubeCon Europe and NorthAmerica. She regularly contributes to the Istio blogs, helping all the latest news about Istio reach users and contributors worldwide.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: Having done the work of an ambassador for Istio for several years now, I look forward to continuing this work officially now, making use of the support CNCF provides. I would love to provide more support for the women in cloud native community with this new opportunity I am provided with.

everyone.name: Mario Fahlandt
everyone.bio: Mario is from a small village in Germany and working for Kubermatic. He studied creative media at an English university and moved his field of knowledge to Cloud Native Infrastructure. He is working as a Customer Delivery Architect with the focus on planning and building concepts and architecture for Infrastructure in the cloud native world. Furthermore, he started the Google Developer Group for Cloud in Munich back in 2016. His passion is always to move the cloud native community one step forward. He is part of the Kubernetes SIG ContribEx and helps in the SIG K8s Infra with reporting.

everyone.name: Veena Gubbi
everyone.bio: Veena is a Principal Platform Engineer focused on building secure, scalable Kubernetes platforms using CNCF technologies. She works extensively with Kubernetes, Kyverno (policy-as-code), Prometheus, Grafana, and Velero, with hands-on experience operating production workloads, backups, disaster recovery, and observability at scale. She is passionate about community learning, mentoring engineers, and fostering practical, production-focused discussions at Kubernetes and cloud-native meetups.

everyone.name: Mauritz Uphoff
everyone.bio: Cloud Solution Architect who is fascinated by the beauty of Kubernetes

everyone.name: John Funk
everyone.bio: DevOps Engineer - I enjoy working with Kubernetes and its rich ecosystem because it provides a powerful, flexible, and scalable way to manage containerized applications. From deploying workloads with Helm and ArgoCD to optimizing resource allocation and securing clusters, I appreciate the balance between automation and fine-grained control. Kubernetes extensibility, combined with tools like Traefik, GitOps workflows, and cloud-native storage solutions, makes it an exciting and ever-evolving space to work in.

ambassadors.name: Abigail Bangser
ambassadors.bio: Abby is a Principal Engineer at Syntasso delivering Kratix, an open-source cloud-native framework for building internal platforms on Kubernetes. Her keen interest in supporting internal development comes from over a decade of experience in consulting and product delivery roles across platform, site reliability, and quality engineering.  Abby is an international keynote speaker, Team Topologies Advocate, and co-host of the #CoffeeOps London meetup. Outside of work, Abby spoils her pup Zino and enjoys playing team sports.

ambassadors.name: Calum Murray
ambassadors.bio: I'm a Knative Eventing Maintainer and UX Working Group Lead. I co-founded the University of Toronto Open Source Students, where I help university students contribute to open source projects, such as Knative and CloudEvents.

ambassadors.name: Marino Wijay
ambassadors.bio: Marino Wijay is a Canadian, Traveller, International Speaker, Open Source Advocate for Service Mesh, CNI, Kubernetes, and Networking. He is an Ambassador @ Civo Cloud, and Lead Organizer for KubeHuddle Toronto. He is passionate about technology and modern distributed systems. He will always fall back to the patterns of Networking and the ways of the OSI. Community building is his driving force; A modern Jedi Academy.

ambassadors.name: Rishit Dagli
ambassadors.bio: Rishit is a CS student at the University of Toronto. He spends most of his time researching and working with learning algorithms and vision. Currently, he also works as a research scientist intern at Qualcomm Research. He also maintains/contributes extensively to popular open-source projects like TensorFlow, PyTorch, Kubernetes, and Kubeflow among others and also frequently works on building new open-source projects. You will also often find him speaking at a CNCF conference.

ambassadors.name: Michael Yao
ambassadors.bio: I'm an active maintainer to kubernetes, Istio, OTel, and more website, a speaker in KubeCon and KCD, and a frequent member of KubeCon Program Committee. I own a technical website about cloud native and AI, and write those content in https://docs.daocloud.io/en/

ambassadors.name: Zou Nengren
ambassadors.bio: I have spent the last seven years honing my expertise in the cloud-native ecosystem, with a particular focus on maintaining various communities like Kubernetes, CoreDNS, KNative, and Containerd. While my experience in these areas has been invaluable, my current focus lies in the Cloud OS System. As a seasoned professional, I regularly share my knowledge and insights on cloud-native technologies through my blog, which can be found at ustack.io. Whether you are a novice or a seasoned pro, my blog posts offer practical tips and in-depth analysis that can help you stay ahead of the curve in this rapidly evolving field. So whether you are looking to dive deeper into Kubernetes or explore the latest trends in cloud-native ecosystem, be sure to check out my blog.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: being an CNCF ambassador provides me with the opportunity to share my knowledge and expertise in cloud-native technologies with a wider audience, and allows me to stay up-to-date with the latest developments and trends in cloud-native technologies

ambassadors.name: Qing Hao
ambassadors.bio: Qing Hao is a senior software engineer at Red Hat. She loves open source and sharing, she is a maintainer of Open Cluster Management and enjoys writing blogs to share new developments in the community. She has been involved in numerous open source activities over the past few years, including being a speaker at KubeCon, a mentor for OSPP and GSoC many times. She has hosted many open source meetups in Xi’an as early as 2015, and she is now one of the organizers of KCD.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I am passionate about open source and sharing, and becoming an ambassador would provide me with more opportunities and motivation to do what I love. I’ve witnessed the CNCF project’s remarkable growth in recent years, and now, with the rise of AI, it’s thriving more than ever. As an ambassador, I look forward to helping more people and projects benefit from open source, which is one of the most exciting aspects of this role.

ambassadors.name: Pranshu Srivastava
ambassadors.bio: Pranshu is a Kubernetes SIG Instrumentation co-chair, a NodeJS emeritus, and a Senior Software Engineer at Red Hat. He has embraced Open Source throughout the years as a mentee, and a mentor, for various code programs. He enjoys helping the cloud ecosystem by building critical APIs, tools, and driving impactful discussions in the Observability space.

kubestronauts.name: Veena Gubbi
kubestronauts.bio: Veena is a Principal Platform Engineer focused on building secure, scalable Kubernetes platforms using CNCF technologies. She works extensively with Kubernetes, Kyverno (policy-as-code), Prometheus, Grafana, and Velero, with hands-on experience operating production workloads, backups, disaster recovery, and observability at scale. She is passionate about community learning, mentoring engineers, and fostering practical, production-focused discussions at Kubernetes and cloud-native meetups.

kubestronauts.name: Leo Paillier
kubestronauts.bio: Strong Cloud architect with a passion for innovation and a focus on DevSecOps. I bring extensive experience in cloud technologies, particularly Kubernetes and Observability. Currently working as an Enterprise Sales Engineer at Datadog where I help customers on their Observability journey. My motto: Always keep learning.

kubestronauts.name: Di Sun
kubestronauts.bio: I am a passionate technologist with a keen focus on Cloud Native Computing technologies. With expertise in Kubernetes, public and hybrid cloud architectures, and DevOps, I thrive in building scalable and resilient systems. I am also enthusiastic about the evolving fields of AI engineering and large language models (LLMs), leveraging them to innovate and create new solutions.

kubestronauts.name: Youngju Kim
kubestronauts.bio: I’m an AI DevOps Engineer at Upstage, previously working as an HBase Engineer with a strong background in distributed systems and data infrastructure. At Upstage, I focus on optimizing and deploying large language models like Solar LLM for scalable production environments.Outside of work, I’m a passionate table tennis lover who enjoys connecting with others who share a love for technology and innovation.

goldenKubestronauts.name: Ana Pedra
goldenKubestronauts.bio: Cloud DevOps Engineer | Golden Kubestronaut 🚀 | Hackathon winner 🏆 and lifelong learner with 40+ certifications across AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, Red Hat, and Kubernetes. Passionate about cloud, Kubernetes, security, and automation — always learning, building, and giving back to the community.

goldenKubestronauts.name: Shunsuke Yoshikawa
goldenKubestronauts.bio: I have several years of experience as an engineer in the Cloud Native domain, primarily focusing on Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS). My expertise lies in the design, construction, and operation of cloud infrastructure. I am also a member of the organizing team for the Platform Engineering Meetup community in Japan.

goldenKubestronauts.name: Majid Faridi
goldenKubestronauts.bio: Majid Faridi is a Senior DevOps/Infrastructure Engineer with over 8 years of experience designing and operating cloud-native platforms at scale. He specializes in Kubernetes, platform engineering, and production-grade infrastructure on AWS. As a Golden Kubestronaut, Majid has achieved all CNCF certifications, demonstrating comprehensive expertise across Kubernetes administration, application development, security, and cloud-native ecosystems. He is passionate about building secure, observable, and highly automated systems, with a strong focus on reducing cognitive load for developers through reliable internal platforms and GitOps-driven workflows. He actively mentors engineers and promotes cloud-native best practices within organizations, enabling teams to build scalable, resilient, and secure platforms.

goldenKubestronauts.name: Gokhan Turhan
goldenKubestronauts.bio: Cross-domain Cloud-Native & Telecom Architect with 18+ years of global experience designing and delivering large-scale IP and 5G Core solutions. Strong background in cloud-native platforms, Kubernetes, and modern networking, combining deep hands-on technical expertise with architectural leadership across presales and delivery. Currently contributing to 5G solution design and integration at Ericsson, focusing on cloud-native environments such as Red Hat OpenShift and Ericsson Cloud Container Distribution. Actively involved in building end-to-end solution architectures, developing proof-of-concepts, creating reusable service assets, and working closely with product development teams to improve solution quality, scalability, and field readiness. Certified across Kubernetes, cloud-native networking, and 5G Core/IP domains, with a strong interest in platform engineering, security-by-design, policy-driven architectures, and AI-ready infrastructure for telecom and distributed systems.
